corpus_metadata.name: Passenger Disability Assistance Service Eligibility Corpus
corpus_metadata.description: Maps passenger disability profiles (IATA SSR codes) to allowed services, equipment handling rules, escort requirements, and regulatory basis. Used as the structured knowledge layer of the Wheelchair Assistance Operations Copilot RAG system.

service_catalog.WHEELCHAIR_TO_GATE: Wheelchair push from check-in/curb to departure gate and from arrival gate to curb
service_catalog.WHEELCHAIR_TO_AIRCRAFT_DOOR: Wheelchair assistance up/down stairs or jet bridge to the aircraft door
service_catalog.WHEELCHAIR_TO_SEAT: Full assistance to the assigned seat using an aisle chair
service_catalog.AISLE_CHAIR_TRANSFER: Onboard aisle chair for boarding, deplaning, and lavatory access
service_catalog.PRIORITY_DEPLANING: Assisted deplaning, typically last off unless connection is tight
service_catalog.MOBILITY_DEVICE_GATE_CHECK: Personal wheelchair/scooter checked at gate and returned at aircraft door on arrival
service_catalog.BATTERY_HANDLING: Dangerous-goods compliant handling of wet/dry/lithium wheelchair batteries
service_catalog.MEET_AND_ASSIST: Staff escort through the terminal without a wheelchair
service_catalog.CONNECTION_ESCORT: Escorted transfer between gates/terminals for connections
service_catalog.SIGN_LANGUAGE_OR_WRITTEN_COMM: Communication of flight info in accessible format (written, visual displays)
service_catalog.SAFETY_BRIEFING_INDIVIDUAL: Individual safety briefing from cabin crew
service_catalog.SERVICE_ANIMAL_RELIEF_AREA: Escort to service animal relief area
service_catalog.ONBOARD_WHEELCHAIR_STOWAGE: Priority in-cabin stowage of one folding manual wheelchair
service_catalog.MEDICAL_OXYGEN_COORDINATION: Coordination of approved portable oxygen concentrator (POC) use
service_catalog.STRETCHER_ARRANGEMENT: Stretcher installation across seat rows with medical escort
service_catalog.EXTENDED_CHECKIN_SUPPORT: Dedicated agent support and extra time at check-in

passenger_profiles.disability_category: Mobility - can walk short distances and climb stairs
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger can ascend/descend stairs and walk to their seat, but cannot walk long distances through the terminal.
seating_restrictions: Not permitted in exit rows per 14 CFR 121.585
passenger_profiles.notes: Most common assistance request (~60% of volume). No advance notice required; airline must provide on request at airport.

passenger_profiles.disability_category: Mobility - cannot climb stairs, can walk to seat
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger cannot ascend or descend stairs but can walk short distances on a level surface to their seat.
passenger_profiles.equipment_notes: Lift or ramp required for remote stands without jet bridge.
passenger_profiles.notes: Requires boarding lift coordination at hardstand gates.

passenger_profiles.description: Passenger is completely immobile and requires assistance from check-in to the aircraft seat, including an aisle chair.
passenger_profiles.escort_conditions: Safety assistant may be required under 14 CFR 382.29 only if passenger cannot assist in their own evacuation.
seating_restrictions: Movable aisle armrest seat required where available per 14 CFR 382.81
passenger_profiles.equipment_notes: Two-agent lift transfer; aisle chair mandatory on aircraft with more than 60 seats per 14 CFR 382.65.
passenger_profiles.notes: Highest-touch service level. Tight-connection rebooking priority applies.

passenger_profiles.disability_category: Mobility - travels with dry/non-spillable battery wheelchair
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger travels with a personal battery-powered wheelchair using a dry or non-spillable battery.
passenger_profiles.advance_notice_reason: Battery handling and cargo door size verification

battery_rules.disconnect_required: Only if device cannot be protected from accidental activation

passenger_profiles.notes: Device returned at aircraft door on arrival unless passenger requests baggage claim delivery.

passenger_profiles.disability_category: Mobility - travels with lithium battery wheelchair
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger travels with a lithium-ion battery powered mobility device.

battery_rules.removable_battery_rule: Removable batteries up to 300 Wh (or 2 x 160 Wh) must be carried in cabin

passenger_profiles.notes: Dispatcher must confirm Wh rating at check-in; flag for DG acceptance check.

passenger_profiles.description: Passenger is blind or has low vision and requires guidance through the airport.
passenger_profiles.excluded_services_note: Wheelchair not provided by default; passenger may decline being placed in one — guidance escort is the correct service.
seating_restrictions: Bulkhead seat offered if traveling with guide dog
passenger_profiles.notes: Common operational error: assigning WCHR instead of MAAS/guidance. Corpus should let RAG flag this.

passenger_profiles.disability_category: Sensory - deaf or hard of hearing
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger is deaf or hard of hearing and requires flight information in visual or written form.
seating_restrictions: Not permitted in exit rows if unable to hear crew commands
passenger_profiles.notes: Gate agents must communicate gate changes, delays, and boarding calls directly, not rely on PA announcements.

passenger_profiles.description: Passenger with an intellectual or developmental disability needing assistance navigating the airport and flight process.
passenger_profiles.escort_conditions: Safety assistant required only if passenger cannot comprehend or respond to safety instructions per 14 CFR 382.29(b)(2).
passenger_profiles.notes: Handoff between agents must be person-to-person; passenger must never be left unattended at transfer points.

passenger_profiles.disability_category: General - meet and assist (elderly, anxiety, temporary injury)
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger needs general escort assistance but no wheelchair, e.g., elderly traveler, first-time flyer with anxiety, or minor temporary injury.
passenger_profiles.notes: Lowest priority tier for dispatch queueing; can be batched with other escorts.

passenger_profiles.description: Passenger requires medical oxygen in flight via an FAA-approved portable oxygen concentrator (POC).
passenger_profiles.advance_notice_reason: Medical clearance and POC battery duration verification (150% of flight duration required)
seating_restrictions: Seat must not block aisle access of others
passenger_profiles.notes: Battery duration check is the most-missed step; corpus should surface the 150% rule prominently.

passenger_profiles.description: Passenger cannot sit upright and must travel on a stretcher installed across seat rows.
passenger_profiles.escort_conditions: Medical escort (physician or nurse) mandatory; travels at passenger expense.
passenger_profiles.notes: Not all aircraft types support stretcher installation; dispatcher must check equipment type. Ambulance coordination at both airports.

passenger_profiles.disability_category: Service animal - trained dog accompanying passenger with disability
passenger_profiles.description: Passenger travels with a trained service dog (psychiatric service dogs included; emotional support animals no longer recognized under US DOT rules since 2021).
documentation_required: DOT Service Animal Air Transportation Form
DOT Relief Attestation Form for flights over 8 hours
seating_restrictions: Animal must fit within passenger foot space
passenger_profiles.notes: Combine with the passenger's underlying disability profile (e.g., BLND + SVAN) — RAG retrieval should merge both records.

business_rules.rule: No advance notice may be required for WCHR, WCHS, WCHC, BLND, DEAF, DPNA, or MAAS services; airlines may only require up to 48 hours notice for the specific services enumerated in 14 CFR 382.27(c).
A passenger may never be charged for any assistance service required under Part 382.
Damaged or lost wheelchairs must be reported as mishandled mobility devices to DOT; airline liability is the original purchase price of the device for domestic travel.
business_rules.citation: 14 CFR 382.131, DOT Air Travel Consumer Report requirements

business_rules.rule: If a connection is under the minimum connect time and the passenger has any wheelchair SSR, dispatch must assign a dedicated (non-batched) escort and notify the receiving gate.
Passengers must not be left unattended in a wheelchair for more than 30 minutes when not independently mobile.
